channel.title: The Climate Digest
channel.description: Your weekly AI-generated briefing on sustainability, climate finance, carbon accounting, and non-financial reporting.
channel.itunes:author: ESG Digest

item.title: The Climate Digest — Episode 7
item.description: This week’s digest covers SBTi’s updated FLAG guidance and Absolute Contraction Approach, the GHG Protocol’s new CEO, a coalition advancing beyond fossil fuels, and new data on tropical deforestation and methane leaks. It also highlights Australia’s Safeguard Mechanism carbon credit demand, ADB’s $70bn cross-border energy push, and Europe’s urban green space inequities.
item.itunes:summary: This week’s digest covers SBTi’s updated FLAG guidance and Absolute Contraction Approach, the GHG Protocol’s new CEO, a coalition advancing beyond fossil fuels, and new data on tropical deforestation and methane leaks. It also highlights Australia’s Safeguard Mechanism carbon credit demand, ADB’s $70bn cross-border energy push, and Europe’s urban green space inequities.

item.title: The Climate Digest — Episode 6
item.description: This week’s digest covers the SBTi’s power sector net-zero standard pilot, corporate calls for the EU to run 50% of its economy on clean power by 2040, new wildfire strategies in Europe, and Malaysia’s push for a palm oil carbon credit framework. It also highlights Colombia’s water sustainability commitment and the US bipartisan geothermal and ocean innovation bills.
item.itunes:summary: This week’s digest covers the SBTi’s power sector net-zero standard pilot, corporate calls for the EU to run 50% of its economy on clean power by 2040, new wildfire strategies in Europe, and Malaysia’s push for a palm oil carbon credit framework. It also highlights Colombia’s water sustainability commitment and the US bipartisan geothermal and ocean innovation bills.

item.title: The Climate Digest — Episode 5
item.description: This week’s Climate Digest covers India’s new 2035 climate commitment, SBTi’s latest governance and FLAG guidance updates, Indonesia’s carbon exchange roadmap, and the ICJ’s climate opinion reshaping legal risks for fossil fuel directors. We also highlight new tools for urban heat mapping and European climate adaptation needs.
item.itunes:summary: This week’s Climate Digest covers India’s new 2035 climate commitment, SBTi’s latest governance and FLAG guidance updates, Indonesia’s carbon exchange roadmap, and the ICJ’s climate opinion reshaping legal risks for fossil fuel directors. We also highlight new tools for urban heat mapping and European climate adaptation needs.

item.title: The Climate Digest — Episode 4
item.description: This week’s Climate Digest covers Microsoft’s abrupt suspension of its carbon removal procurement program, Australia’s accelerated push to reform its ACCU scheme, and the EU’s new wildfire resilience strategy. We also examine the latest trends in corporate climate target-setting and the shipping sector’s tax avoidance on carbon pricing.
item.itunes:summary: This week’s Climate Digest covers Microsoft’s abrupt suspension of its carbon removal procurement program, Australia’s accelerated push to reform its ACCU scheme, and the EU’s new wildfire resilience strategy. We also examine the latest trends in corporate climate target-setting and the shipping sector’s tax avoidance on carbon pricing.

item.title: The Climate Digest — Week 15, 2026
item.description: This week’s Digest covers India’s revised 2035 climate targets, SBTi’s governance and FLAG guidance updates, new EU climate adaptation insights, and market shifts in carbon removal funding and blue carbon integrity. We also highlight US legislative moves on geothermal and ocean innovation.
item.itunes:summary: This week’s Digest covers India’s revised 2035 climate targets, SBTi’s governance and FLAG guidance updates, new EU climate adaptation insights, and market shifts in carbon removal funding and blue carbon integrity. We also highlight US legislative moves on geothermal and ocean innovation.

item.title: The Climate Digest — Week 14, 2026
item.description: This week’s Climate Digest covers SBTi’s sector-specific net-zero standards for power and chemicals, China’s nuclear offset methodology under CCER, EU biodiversity funding via EIB, and market movements in WCI allowances. Regulatory shifts in the US and personnel changes at WRI also feature.
item.itunes:summary: This week’s Climate Digest covers SBTi’s sector-specific net-zero standards for power and chemicals, China’s nuclear offset methodology under CCER, EU biodiversity funding via EIB, and market movements in WCI allowances. Regulatory shifts in the US and personnel changes at WRI also feature.

item.title: The Climate Digest — Week 13, 2026
item.description: India raises its 2035 climate ambition with new targets, SBTi updates FLAG guidance and launches automotive net-zero consultation, and blue carbon markets face a critical tension between scientific rigor and scalability. Meanwhile, Europe accelerates adaptation with early warning systems and AI-driven climate tools.
item.itunes:summary: India raises its 2035 climate ambition with new targets, SBTi updates FLAG guidance and launches automotive net-zero consultation, and blue carbon markets face a critical tension between scientific rigor and scalability. Meanwhile, Europe accelerates adaptation with early warning systems and AI-driven climate tools.
